name: RvD1 improved aged mouse muscle functional recovery after injury
summary: Aged muscle showed deficient pro-resolving mediator biosynthesis after injury, and systemic RvD1 improved recovery of muscle-specific force while limiting fibrosis and inflammatory cytokine expression.
statement: After skeletal-muscle injury, aged mice had deficient local SPM-pathway biosynthesis; daily systemic RvD1 suppressed inflammatory cytokine expression, limited fibrosis, and improved recovery of muscle-specific force, although it did not fully restore leukocyte recruitment or myofiber regeneration.
population_or_model: Aged female C57BL/6 mice with barium-chloride tibialis anterior injury
time_horizon: Short-term injury recovery after daily systemic RvD1 treatment
quantitative_note: The staged baseline treats this as animal functional support with explicit regeneration limits.
caveats: RvD1 improved force recovery and remodeling but did not fully rescue myofiber regeneration.
The injury model does not establish broad healthy-aging or LEV-relevant benefit.
